result.generated_article:
==Geography==
Hawaii is composed of eight major islands and 124 minor islands covering a total land area of 6,425 square miles.[4]
The archipelago's general coastline measures approximately 750 miles.[4]
Mauna Kea, the highest peak, rises to 13,796 feet above sea level.[4]
The longest river, Kaukonahua Stream on Oʻahu, is 33 miles in length.[4]
The largest lake, Halaliʻi on Niʻihau, covers 841 acres.[4]
The biggest named waterfall, Kahiwa on Molokaʻi, drops 1,750 feet.[4]
Honolulu International Airport is about 2,397 miles from San Francisco and 4,829 miles from Washington, D.C.[5]
Distances between the islands and other locations are documented in geographic tables.[5]

==Tourism==
Air arrivals to Hawaii reached 9,827,1
